description: In column A; report the adjusted carrying value of the bank's equity exposures subject to a 600 percent risk weight under paragraph (b)(6) of section 52 of the final rule. In column B; report 600 percent of the amount in column A for this item.

item_name: EQUITY EXPOSURES TO INVESTMENT FUNDS: FULL LOOK-THROUGH APPROACH
description: In column A; report the adjusted carrying value of all equity exposures to investment funds to which the bank applies the full look-through approach as described in paragraph (b) of section 54 of the final rule. In column B; report the risk weighted assets of the amount in column A for this item.

item_name: EQUITY EXPOSURES TO INVESTMENT FUNDS: SIMPLE MODIFIED LOOK-THROUGH APPROACH
description: In column A; report the adjusted carrying value of all equity exposures to investment funds to which the bank applies the simple modified look-through approach as described in paragraph (c) of section 54 of the final rule. In column B; report the risk weighted assets for the amount in column A for this item.

item_name: EQUITY EXPOSURES TO INVESTMENT FUNDS: ALTERNATIVE MODIFIED LOOK-THROUGH APPROACH
description: In column A; report the adjusted carrying value of all equity exposures to investment funds for which the bank applies the alternative modified look-through approach as described in paragraph (d) of section 54 of the final rule. In column B; report the risk weighted assets for the amount in column A for this item.

item_name: EQUITY EXPOSURES TO INVESTMENT FUNDS: MONEY MARKET FUND APPROACH
description: In column A; report the adjusted carrying value of all equity exposures to investment funds that qualify for the Money Market Fund Approach described in paragraph (e) of section 54 of the final rule. In column B; report 7 percent of the amount in column A for this item.

item_name: TOTAL RISK WEIGHTED ASSETS FOR INVESTMENT FUNDS
description: In column B; report the sum of amounts in column B; items 11 through 14.

item_name: ESTIMATE OF POTENTIAL LOSSES ON PUBLICLY-TRADED EQUITY
description: In column A; report the estimated potential losses on the bank's publicly traded equity exposures; excluding those reported in column A; items 2; 3; 4; 8; and 9 of this schedule; and equity exposures to investment funds. In column B; report 12.5 times the amount in column A for this item.

item_name: FLOORS PARTIAL INTERNAL MODELS APPROACH: PUBLICLY TRADED
description: In column A; report sum of (i) the aggregated adjusted carrying value of the bank's publicly traded equity exposures that do not belong to a hedge pair; are not reported in column A; items 2 through 4 of this schedule; and are not equity exposures to an investment fund; and (ii) the ineffective portion of all hedge pairs. In column B; report 200 percent of the amount in column A for this item.

item_name: TOTAL RISK WEIGHTED ASSETS -- PARTIAL INTERNAL MODELS APPROACH
description: In column B; report the larger of column B; item 23 or column B; item 24.

item_name: TOTAL: PARTIAL INTERNAL MODELS APPROACH; PARTIAL SIMPLE RISK WEIGHT APPROACH
description: In column B; report the sum of column B; items 3; 4; 8; 9; 15 and 25.

item_name: CONFIDENTIAL DATA INDICATOR FOR SCHEDULE S
description: Code indicating whether a reporter has requested confidential treatment for schedule S BHCKC050: CONFIDENTIAL DATA INDICATOR FOR THE FR Y-9C SECURITIZATION AND ASSET SALE ACTIVITIES AASAC050: FFIEC101 Schedule S is 'Operational Risk'

item_name: OPERATIONAL RISK CAPITAL: RISK-BASED CAPITAL REQUIREMENT FOR OPERATIONAL RISK
description: Report the dollar amount of the risk-based capital requirement for operational risk pursuant to the requirements of the final rule.

item_name: OPERATIONAL RISK CAPITAL: IS ITEM 1 GENERATED FROM AN 'ALTERNATIVE OPERATIONAL RISK QUANTIFICATION SYSTEM?'
description: Report whether the risk-based capital figure reported in item 1 results from an 'alternative operational risk quantification system' (as discussed in section 22(h)(3)(ii) of the final rule) by indicating '1' for (yes) or '0' for (no) for this item.

Report the dollar amount of the expected value of the distribution of potential aggregate operational losses; as generated by the bank's operational risk quantification system using a one-year horizon.

item_name: TOTAL ELIGIBLE OPERATIONAL RISK OFFSETS: ELIGIBLE GAAP RESERVES
description: Report the dollar amount of reserves calculated in a manner consistent with GAAP.

item_name: TOTAL ELIGIBLE OPERATIONAL RISK OFFSETS: OTHER ELIGIBLE OFFSETS
description: Report the dollar amount of offsets approved by the institution's supervisor outside of GAAP reserves reported in Eligible GAAP reserves (item 4a).

item_name: TOTAL RISK-BASED CAPITAL REQUIREMENT FOR OPERATIONAL RISK WITHOUT: DEPENDENCE ASSUMPTIONS
description: Report the risk-based capital requirement for operational risk excluding the effects of dependence assumptions.

item_name: TOTAL RISK-BASED CAPITAL REQUIREMENT FOR OPERATIONAL RISK WITHOUT: ADJUSTMENTS REFLECTING BUSINESS ENVIRONMENT AND INTERNAL CONTROL FACTORS
description: Report the risk-based capital requirement for operational risk excluding the effects of qualitative adjustments that account for business environment and internal control factors.

item_name: TOTAL RISK-BASED CAPITAL REQUIREMENT FOR OPERATIONAL RISK WITHOUT: RISK MITIGANTS (E.G.; INSURANCE)
description: Report the risk-based capital requirement for operational risk excluding the effects of qualifying operational risk mitigants; as discussed in section 61 of the final rule.

item_name: DATE RANGES OF INTERNAL OPERATIONAL LOSS EVENT DATA USED IN MODELING OPERATIONAL RISK CAPITAL: STARTING DATE FOR FREQUENCY DISTRIBUTION
description: Report the earliest date relevant to the internal operational loss event data used in modeling the frequency distribution for operational risk capital. If such a distribution is not used; report 'N/A' for this item. Date should be expressed in the MM/YYYY.

item_name: DATE RANGES OF INTERNAL OPERATIONAL LOSS EVENT DATA USED IN MODELING OPERATIONAL RISK CAPITAL: ENDING DATE FOR FREQUENCY DISTRIBUTION
description: Report the latest date relevant to the internal operational loss event data used in modeling the frequency distribution for operational risk capital. If such a distribution is not used; report 'N/A' for this item. Date should be expressed in the MM/YYYY.

item_name: DATE RANGES OF INTERNAL OPERATIONAL LOSS EVENT DATA USED IN MODELING OPERATIONAL RISK CAPITAL: STARTING DATE FOR SEVERITY DISTRIBUTION
description: Report the earliest date relevant to the internal operational loss event data used in modeling the severity distribution for operational risk capital. If such a distribution is not used; report 'N/A' for this item. Date should be expressed in the MM/YYYY.

item_name: DATE RANGES OF INTERNAL OPERATIONAL LOSS EVENT DATA USED IN MODELING OPERATIONAL RISK CAPITAL: ENDING DATE FOR SEVERITY DISTRIBUTION
description: Report the latest date relevant to the internal operational loss event data used in modeling the severity distribution for operational risk capital. If such a distribution is not used; report 'N/A' for this item. Date should be expressed in the MM/YYYY.

item_name: INTERNAL OPERATIONAL LOSS EVENT DATA CHARACTERISTICS: HIGHEST DOLLAR THRESHOLD APPLIED IN MODELING INTERNAL OPERATIONAL LOSS EVENT DATA
description: Report the dollar threshold that is used to exclude smaller internal operational loss event data from operational risk capital modeling. If more than one threshold is applied in the modeling process; report the highest threshold used. If no thresholds are used; report '0' for this item.

item_name: INTERNAL OPERATIONAL LOSS EVENT DATA CHARACTERISTICS: DOES THE DOLLAR THRESHOLD CHANGE ACROSS UNITS OF MEASURE?
description: Report whether the thresholds for the internal loss data used in modeling operational risk capital differ across units of measure by indicating '1' for (yes) or '0' for (no) for this item. As defined in the final rule; unit of measure is the level (for example; organizational unit or operational loss event type) at which the bank's operational risk quantification system generates a separate distribution of potential operational losses.

Report the total number of internal loss events used in the model to determine the risk-based capital requirement for operational risk. A loss event may encompass one loss transaction or may comprise multiple loss transactions all related to the same event. For example; individual losses of $2;000; $6;000; and $12;000 that all relate to a single loss event should be considered one loss (amounting to $20;000) for purposes of calculating this item. description: Report the dollar value of the largest single internal loss event used in the model to determine the risk-based capital requirement for operational risk. The largest internal loss event should include all the loss transactions related to the single event.

description: Report the total number of units of measure for which the statistical distribution(s) used this reporting period to estimate loss frequency differs from those used in the prior reporting period. This refers to changes in the distribution type. If frequency distributions are not used; report 'N/A' for this item.

item_name: SEVERITY DISTRIBUTION: ACROSS HOW MANY INDIVIDUAL UNITS OF MEASURE DID THE CHOICE OF SEVERITY DISTRIBUTION CHANGE SINCE THE LAST REPORTING PERIOD (IF APPLICABLE)?

This refers to changes in the distribution type. If frequency distributions are not used; report 'N/A' for this item.

item_name: HOW MANY LOSS CAPS ARE USED IN CALCULATING THE RISK-BASED CAPITAL REQUIREMENT FOR OPERATIONAL RISK?
description: Report the number of loss caps used to limit loss size in the quantification process for determining the risk-based capital requirement for operational risk. If loss caps are not used; report '0' for this item.

item_name: WHAT IS THE DOLLAR AMOUNT OF THE SMALLEST CAP USED (IF APPLICABLE)?

item_name: WHAT IS THE DOLLAR AMOUNT OF THE LARGEST CAP USED (IF APPLICABLE)?

The Legal Entity Identifier (LEI) is a 20-digit alpha-numeric reference code to uniquely identify a legally distinct entity that engages in a financial transaction. The purpose for the LEI is to have a unified global identification system for legal entities across markets and jurisdictions. The LEI will be a linchpin for financial data-the first global and unique entity identifier enabling risk managers and regulators to identify parties to financial transactions instantly and precisely.
